verdict_reason: P1 factual and prerequisite issues remain: the upload fixtures named by the corpus are absent, the PDF parse/chunk expectations do not match the current upload substrate, and the bundle field expectations do not match the EvidenceContract JSON returned by the Phase 1 bundle endpoint.

findings.description: The briefing lists required fixture PDFs under tests/v6/fixtures/walkthrough/1.8_*.pdf while also saying they are not committed yet. Test-Path confirms the walkthrough fixture directory and the three named PDFs are absent, so evaluators cannot execute the F3 upload block consistently.

Inputs #11 and #12 expect uploaded PDFs to parse and show chunk lists, but src/polaris_v6/api/upload.py only produces chunk_preview for .md/.txt files; PDFs return parse_status queued with no chunks. This would make a correct Phase 1 substrate look broken.

Input #14 says the EvidenceContract tier enum is T1-T7 plus UNKNOWN and user uploads map to T7, but src/polaris_v6/schemas/evidence_contract.py currently defines SourceSpan.source_tier as only T1, T2, or T3. The evaluator is being told to expect an impossible schema value.

Input #16 says EvidenceContract required fields include manifest_version, report_md, evidence[], trace_jsonl, and provenance, but the current schema uses contract_version, evidence_pool, verified_sentences, frame_coverage, contradictions, and related run metadata. The F15 check would mis-grade the actual JSON bundle.

The briefing repeats the same incorrect F15 required fields as the corpus. Since src/polaris_v6/api/bundle.py returns an EvidenceContract response model, the evaluator orientation is factually wrong before they reach the detailed script.

Input #20 says run #1 should have at least one flagged contradiction, but the available clinical golden bundle golden_clinical_001 has an empty contradictions array and is an Ozempic query, not the tirzepatide flow from #1. The contradiction check needs a deterministic fixture or run target.
